title: Group-CV rank-stacked model family blend
summary: Current solutions rely mainly on AutoGluon GBM/XGB under random holdout.
rationale: Current solutions rely mainly on AutoGluon GBM/XGB under random holdout. Public tabular gains often come from diverse learners and validation that detects group leakage; CatBoost also handles categorical combinations natively.
implementation_hint: Build OOF predictions under Race-Year GroupKFold for LightGBM, XGBoost, CatBoost, and a simple logistic/hazard baseline. Train a shallow meta-model or rank-average only models that improve both random holdout and group CV. If calibrating, prefer sigmoid over isotonic when preserving AUC ranking is important.
expected_effect: Expected to improve public robustness more than local random CV by reducing race memorization and adding categorical model diversity.
risk: Group CV scores may look worse and slow search; rank averaging can dilute a single best model if candidates are too correlated.
